title: Ike's Morning Digest
data_note: Built from publisher RSS metadata (titles + teaser text). `coverage_window_hours` is the default (40h); several feeds use a longer `rollingHours` (see `scripts/build-digest.mjs`) so rows align with what is still on each publisher’s listing pages. Commercial Observer keeps its retail category + hub supplement; TRD adds a New York–filtered global retail tag feed. PincusCo uses the live site feed only (`/category/retail/feed/` is stale). **CRE Daily** retail briefs come from `credaily.com/sectors/retail/feed/`. Open each link for the full article (subscriptions may apply in the browser).

section_overviews.commercial_observer:
Luxury Jewelry Brand David Yurman Takes 685 Fifth’s Entire 23K-SF Retail Condo: Luxury jewelry retailer David Yurman has leased the entire retail condominium at 685 Fifth Avenue , just one month after a $160 million loan on the property was transferred to special servicing.

Fred Astaire Dance Studios Leases First Park Slope Location: The international dance franchise Fred Astaire Dance Studios is waltzing into Brooklyn’s Park Slope neighborhood. The school’s new lease at 508 Fifth Avenue spans 2,500 square feet of ground-floor retail space previously occupied by shoe store Bobby Sportswear .

Cashmere Brand Kujten Picks Acadia’s 92 Greene Street for Second U.S. Location: French cashmere brand Kujten has leased a second Manhattan location less than a year after opening its first U.S. flagship along Madison Avenue.

Bistro Pardon My French Sets Up Shop at Jeff Sutton’s 373 Broome Street: Ooh la la. French-inspired bistro Pardon My French is coming to Manhattan’s SoHo neighborhood after signing a 1,000-square-foot lease at 373 Broome Street , a property owned in a joint venture between Jeff Sutton ’s Wharton Properties and Gorjian Real Estate Group, according to broker Meridian Capital Group .
section_overviews.cre_daily:
Blackstone Lends $125M Refi to Dallas Mixed-Use Complex: Harbor Group International landed a $125M refinancing from Blackstone for the Alesio Urban Center in Irving, Texas. The 908-unit mixed-use property includes 55,500 SF of retail and recently underwent a $4.5M value-add program.

Drive-Thru Traffic Slides, Challenging Net Lease Retail: Short-duration, drive-thru visits to quick-service restaurants fell 6.8% year-over-year in May per Placer.ai. Full-service restaurants and experiential dining gained momentum even as drive-thru traffic declined.

US Core Retail Sales Hit Record as Shoppers Shift Online: Core retail sales increased 0.5% month-over-month and 5.6% year-over-year in May, reflecting resilient consumer spending. Online and discount retailers saw record sales, while restaurant and electronics spending slowed as households cut back on discretionary purchases.

Toys and Collectibles Fuel Booming US Retail Foot Traffic: Toys and collectibles are rapidly boosting foot traffic and sales for US retailers, drawing demand from Gen Z, Millennials, and older buyers alike. Brands employ scarcity tactics and viral social trends, while global companies like Pop Mart and Miniso expand their US retail footprints to capitalize on the craze.
section_overviews.new_york_post:
Billionaire tech CEO latest to ditch California with $18M sale of sprawling ranch: Former eBay CEO Meg Whitman has unloaded her 1,530-acre Fall River Mills ranch for $17.9M. In 2025, she and her husband sold a penthouse atop a downtown Sacramento hotel for nearly $2.4 million.

Revealed: The worst place to sell a home in NYC — where homeowners lose a median $24K: New York City homeowners who sold in 2025 walked away with a median gain of $70,000, but that windfall masked a tale of two markets, per PropertyShark.

Dallas OKs incentive package for $1.3B Morgan Stanley office tower — and far-left election wins in NYC could spark more businesses to leave: Officials rubber-stamped the deal that would grant the New York-based bank a special economic grant and up to a decade of business personal property tax abatements.

San Francisco’s $750 apartment is nightmare fuel for our robot overlord future: It's like a tiny doctor's office waiting room — but the nurse never comes to save you.
section_overviews.pincusco:
Stockbridge Capital Group pays $46.1M to Nuveen for retail in Yorkville: San Francisco-based Stockbridge Capital Group through the entity SCG 1511 Third Avenue, LLC paid $46.1.

Lonicera Partners signs $150M loan with PNC for 314-unit rental in Downtown Brooklyn: Lonicera Partners through the entity Hanover Tower Owner LLC as borrower signed a loan with.

Poise Property Group signs $16.2M construction loan for 20-unit dev site in Greenpoint: Poise Property Group through the entity 55 Eckford St LLC as borrower signed a initial.

EMP Capital Group files plans for 79 units in Astoria: EMP Capital Group submitted a new building construction project for a 79-unit, 85,283-square-foot residential (R-2).
section_overviews.the_real_deal:
Nassau rent board approves modest increases for regulated apartments: As a rent freeze looms over New York City, rent-stabilized tenants in Nassau County dodged steep hikes this year but stopped short of a solid freeze. The Nassau County Rent Guidelines Board voted 5-2 on Wednesday to approve rent increases of up to 1.5 percent for one-year lease renewals and 2.5 percent for two-year.

TA Realty takes rare retail dive on Staten Island: Boston-based TA Realty largely dabbles in the industrial sector, but is taking a chance on a pair of retail properties on Staten Island. James Raisides’ firm acquired two buildings in the borough’s New Dorp Beach neighborhood for a combined $79.2 million, the Commercial Observer reported.

HGI fills famed Black Rock tower with 170K sf law firm lease: Harbor Group International’s multimillion-dollar bet on the Black Rock building has paid off. International law firm Alston & Bird signed a 170,000-square-foot lease at HGI’s 51 West 52nd Street, also known as 51W52 and Black Rock, bringing the Midtown trophy tower to full occupancy.

It’s official: New York City is getting a rent freeze: New York City is freezing the rents of rent-stabilized apartments. The city’s Rent Guidelines Board voted seven to one in favor of a freeze Thursday night, at El Museo del Barrio in Manhattan.

items.headline: Luxury Jewelry Brand David Yurman Takes 685 Fifth’s Entire 23K-SF Retail Condo
items.summary: Luxury jewelry retailer David Yurman has leased the entire retail condominium at 685 Fifth Avenue , just one month after a $160 million loan on the property was transferred to special servicing. The jewelry brand will open a new 23,000-square-foot David Yurman flagship store at the base of the 29-story Midtown building, according to landlord GGP , the retail subsidiary of Brookfield Corporation .…

items.headline: Fred Astaire Dance Studios Leases First Park Slope Location
items.summary: The international dance franchise Fred Astaire Dance Studios is waltzing into Brooklyn’s Park Slope neighborhood. The school’s new lease at 508 Fifth Avenue spans 2,500 square feet of ground-floor retail space previously occupied by shoe store Bobby Sportswear . The 10-year deal’s asking rent was $72 per square foot. The lease is the New York dance studio franchise’s sixth deal completed by Lee & Associates NYC ‘s Claude Kolb .…

items.headline: Cashmere Brand Kujten Picks Acadia’s 92 Greene Street for Second U.S. Location
items.summary: French cashmere brand Kujten has leased a second Manhattan location less than a year after opening its first U.S. flagship along Madison Avenue. The upcoming store at Acadia Realty Trust ’s 92 Greene Street , a midblock address bordered by Spring and Prince streets, sits in the heart of SoHo’s bustling shopping corridor. Kujten signed a long-term, 2,567-square-foot lease, with 1,531 square feet on the ground floor and 1,036 square feet on the lower level, according to landlord broker Newmark .…

items.headline: Bistro Pardon My French Sets Up Shop at Jeff Sutton’s 373 Broome Street
items.summary: Ooh la la. French-inspired bistro Pardon My French is coming to Manhattan’s SoHo neighborhood after signing a 1,000-square-foot lease at 373 Broome Street , a property owned in a joint venture between Jeff Sutton ’s Wharton Properties and Gorjian Real Estate Group, according to broker Meridian Capital Group . The asking rent for the ground-floor retail space was $225 per square foot. The length of the lease, which was signed at the end of April, was not disclosed.…

items.headline: Blackstone Lends $125M Refi to Dallas Mixed-Use Complex
items.summary: Harbor Group International landed a $125M refinancing from Blackstone for the Alesio Urban Center in Irving, Texas. The 908-unit mixed-use property includes 55,500 SF of retail and recently underwent a $4.5M value-add program. The deal underscores lender appetite for stable, large-scale multifamily assets in the Dallas market despite national debt market volatility.…

items.headline: Drive-Thru Traffic Slides, Challenging Net Lease Retail
items.summary: Short-duration, drive-thru visits to quick-service restaurants fell 6.8% year-over-year in May per Placer.ai. Full-service restaurants and experiential dining gained momentum even as drive-thru traffic declined. CRE investors should reexamine assumptions about outparcel and net lease assets anchored by pure convenience demand.…

items.headline: US Core Retail Sales Hit Record as Shoppers Shift Online
items.summary: Core retail sales increased 0.5% month-over-month and 5.6% year-over-year in May, reflecting resilient consumer spending. Online and discount retailers saw record sales, while restaurant and electronics spending slowed as households cut back on discretionary purchases. Absorption in retail real estate turned positive in Q2 2026, driven by demand for big-box and value-focused tenants.…

items.headline: Toys and Collectibles Fuel Booming US Retail Foot Traffic
items.summary: Toys and collectibles are rapidly boosting foot traffic and sales for US retailers, drawing demand from Gen Z, Millennials, and older buyers alike. Brands employ scarcity tactics and viral social trends, while global companies like Pop Mart and Miniso expand their US retail footprints to capitalize on the craze. The collectibles surge is reshaping retail leasing dynamics—offering landlords, developers, and brokers new opportunities for high-frequency, intent-driven tenants.…

items.headline: Nassau rent board approves modest increases for regulated apartments
items.summary: As a rent freeze looms over New York City, rent-stabilized tenants in Nassau County dodged steep hikes this year but stopped short of a solid freeze. The Nassau County Rent Guidelines Board voted 5-2 on Wednesday to approve rent increases of up to 1.5 percent for one-year lease renewals and 2.5 percent for two-year renewals at regulated apartments, according to Newsday. The rates take effect Oct. 1 and apply to the county’s roughly 8,100 rent-stabilized units.…

items.headline: TA Realty takes rare retail dive on Staten Island
items.summary: Boston-based TA Realty largely dabbles in the industrial sector, but is taking a chance on a pair of retail properties on Staten Island. James Raisides’ firm acquired two buildings in the borough’s New Dorp Beach neighborhood for a combined $79.2 million, the Commercial Observer reported. The deal for two properties breaks down to roughly $430 per built square foot. An affiliate of the Bregman Organization, led by Paul Bregman, sold the buildings at 2712 Hylan Boulevard and 2754 Hylan Boulevard.…

items.headline: Design Firm Décor Global Inks 7K-SF Lease at 231 West 39th Street
items.summary: Manhattan’s Garment District has sewn up yet another design tenant. Décor Global , an apparel, accessory design and manufacturing company, signed a lease spanning 6,973 square feet at 231 West 39th Street , Commercial Observer has learned. The 12-story office building is owned by the LLC 231/249 West 39 Street Associates . Décor Global will use its new space for offices as well as a showroom for wholesale apparel sales, according to landlord broker and building manager Adams & Company .…

items.headline: Union Investment, Nuveen Sell Retail Property at 1511 Third Avenue for $46M
items.summary: Union Investment and Nuveen Real Estate have offloaded a retail property on Manhattan’s Upper East Side for $46.1 million, property records made public Wednesday show. The joint venture sold 1511 Third Avenue , a four-story retail building in the Upper East Side’s Yorkville neighborhood, to San Francisco-based real estate investment management firm Stockbridge , according to the sellers. The sale is Stockbridge’s second overall transaction and first retail deal in New York City, a source close to the deal said.…

items.headline: Wells Fargo Provides $46M Refi for SoCal Shopping Center
items.summary: A Target -anchored shopping center in Southern California’s San Gabriel Valley has secured refinancing, Walker & Dunlop announced Thursday. Wells Fargo provided $46 million in fixed-rate, interest-only permanent financing for the 336,718-square-foot Rosemead Place in Rosemead, Calif., in Los Angeles County. PropertyShark lists MPM Partners , run by Herbert Yang , as the property’s owner. The shopping center is about 10 miles east of Downtown L.A.…

items.headline: HGI fills famed Black Rock tower with 170K sf law firm lease
items.summary: Harbor Group International’s multimillion-dollar bet on the Black Rock building has paid off. International law firm Alston & Bird signed a 170,000-square-foot lease at HGI’s 51 West 52nd Street, also known as 51W52 and Black Rock, bringing the Midtown trophy tower to full occupancy. The firm inked a 15-year deal for floors nine through 15 at the roughly 900,000-square-foot Class A office tower, according to an announcement from Newmark.…

items.headline: US Small Business Tenants Face Profit Pressures in 2026
items.summary: Small business tenants are experiencing falling profitability, with a 1.3% year-over-year drop per Bank of America Institute’s May 2026 data. The war in Iran and surging energy costs have driven up construction and operating expenses, challenging lease renewal and expansion plans. CRE landlords could soon see higher vacancy and default rates from their largest tenant segment as financial stress accumulates.…

items.headline: It’s official: New York City is getting a rent freeze
items.summary: New York City is freezing the rents of rent-stabilized apartments. The city’s Rent Guidelines Board voted seven to one in favor of a freeze Thursday night, at El Museo del Barrio in Manhattan. The board approved rent increases of 0 percent on one-year leases and 0 percent on two-year leases The result delivers on Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s campaign promise and is a show of strength for the tenant advocates who helped elect him. For landlord groups the vote was a difficult, if expected, blow.…

items.headline: Fitness Factory to Open 25K-SF Gym at 162-11 Jamaica Avenue in Queens
items.summary: Don’t you know pump it up? You got to pump it up! And residents in Queens are about to get another option for pumping up their gym workout. Fitness Factory , a growing fitness and wellness chain in New York and New Jersey, has signed a 25,000-square-foot lease to open a new gym at 162-11 Jamaica Avenue in Jamaica, Queens, Commercial Observer has learned. The deal was signed June 18, according to a source close to the deal. Fitness Factory will open a four-floor gym at its new space.…

items.headline: Law Firm Alston & Bird Takes 170K SF at 51 West 52nd Street
items.summary: International law firm Alston & Bird has signed a major deal to relocate its offices from Park Avenue. Alston & Bird, which provides legal counsel to Fortune 100 companies and private institutions, signed a 15-year, 169,664-square-foot lease at Harbor Group International ’s 51 West 52nd Street , with plans to move its operations from 90 Park Avenue , according to landlord broker Newmark .…

items.headline: Stockbridge Capital Group pays $46.1M to Nuveen for retail in Yorkville
items.summary: San Francisco-based Stockbridge Capital Group through the entity SCG 1511 Third Avenue, LLC paid $46.1 The post Stockbridge Capital Group pays $46.1M to Nuveen for retail in Yorkville appeared first on PincusCo .

items.headline: Walmart Pilots Dark Store Depots For Faster Last-Mile Delivery
items.summary: Walmart has launched Spark Delivery Depots—small, closed-to-public fulfillment hubs—starting in Fayetteville, Arkansas. Repurposing 20,000 SF former drugstores, these dark stores focus on stocking high-demand items for rapid local delivery at a premium fee. This model leverages Walmart’s store footprint to challenge Amazon’s last-mile advantage and accelerate e-commerce growth in the US.…

items.headline: French Cafe L’Ami Pierre to Open at Midtown’s 400 Madison Avenue
items.summary: A French cafe by acclaimed restaurateur Pierre-Antoine Raberin is opening a second location in Midtown, Commercial Observer has learned. Raberin has signed a 2,400-square-foot lease at 400 Madison Avenue for the second outpost of L’Ami Pierre , according to tenant broker Judson CRE . The deal follows the exit of Raberin’s partner, Michelin -starred chef Eric Ripert , in May 2023 .…

items.headline: NYC’s top deals: GO Residential closes on Ivy Tower in Yorkville for $148M
items.summary: There were 302 transactions totaling $641 million filed in New York City records in the 24 hours before 4 p.m. on Thursday, June 25. Commercial: Midtown had the top commercial real estate deal to hit records, with the sale of two apartment complexes known as the Ivy Tower at 349 West 42nd Street and 350 West 43rd Street for $148 million. The property on 42nd Street stands 41 stories tall and has 268 apartments; the 43rd Street building stands seven stories tall and houses 52 apartments.…

items.headline: Lonicera Partners signs $150M loan with PNC for 314-unit rental in Downtown Brooklyn
items.summary: Lonicera Partners through the entity Hanover Tower Owner LLC as borrower signed a loan with The post Lonicera Partners signs $150M loan with PNC for 314-unit rental in Downtown Brooklyn appeared first on PincusCo .

items.summary: Poise Property Group through the entity 55 Eckford St LLC as borrower signed a initial The post Poise Property Group signs $16.2M construction loan for 20-unit dev site in Greenpoint appeared first on PincusCo .

items.headline: Billionaire tech CEO latest to ditch California with $18M sale of sprawling ranch
items.summary: Former eBay CEO Meg Whitman has unloaded her 1,530-acre Fall River Mills ranch for $17.9M. In 2025, she and her husband sold a penthouse atop a downtown Sacramento hotel for nearly $2.4 million.

items.headline: Developer looks to bring almost 300 units to Flushing
items.summary: Flushing is poised to receive an influx of nearly 300 units — many affordable — should Gary Chen secure a zoning change. On Monday, Chen filed an application with the New York City Department of City Planning for a mixed-use project at 32-02 Linden Place in the Queens neighborhood, the Commercial Observer reported. The zoning change would be bound by 32nd Avenue, Linden Place and Farrington Street.…

items.headline: Revealed: The worst place to sell a home in NYC — where homeowners lose a median $24K
items.summary: New York City homeowners who sold in 2025 walked away with a median gain of $70,000, but that windfall masked a tale of two markets, per PropertyShark.

items.summary: Officials rubber-stamped the deal that would grant the New York-based bank a special economic grant and up to a decade of business personal property tax abatements.
